classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “This beginning of miracles did Jesus in Cana of Galilee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Frequent and fierce is the devil in his attacks, on all sides besieging our salvation; we therefore must watch and be sober, and everywhere fortify ourselves against his assault, for if he but gain some slight vantage ground, he goes on to make for himself a broad passage, and by degrees introduces all his forces.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If then we have any care at all for our salvation, let us not allow him to make his approaches even in trifles, that thus we may check him beforehand in important matters; for it would be the extreme of folly, if, while he displays such eagerness to destroy our souls, we should not bring even an equal amount in defense of our own salvation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I say not this without a cause, but because I fear lest that wolf be even now standing unseen by us in the midst of the fold, and some sheep become a prey to him, being led astray from the flock and from hearkening by its own carelessness and his craft.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Were the wounds sensible, or did the body receive the blows, there would be no difficulty in discerning his plots; but since the soul is invisible, and since that it is which receives the wounds, we need great watchfulness that each may prove himself; for none knoweth the things of a man as the spirit of a man that is in him. ( 1 Cor. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The word is spoken indeed to all, and is offered as a general remedy to those who need it, but it is the business of every individual hearer to take what is suited to his complaint.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I know not who are sick, I know not who are well.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore I use every sort of argument, and introduce remedies suited to all maladies, at one time condemning covetousness, after that touching on luxury, and again on impurity, then composing something in praise of and exhortation to charity, and each of the other virtues in their turn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For I fear lest when my arguments are employed on any one subject, I may without knowing it be treating you for one disease while you are ill of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So that if this congregation were but one person, I should not have judged it so absolutely necessary to make my discourse varied; but since in such a multitude there are probably also many maladies, I not unreasonably diversify my teaching, since my discourse will be sure to attain its object when it is made to embrace you all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this cause also Scripture is something multiform, and speaks on ten thousand matters, because it addresses itself to the nature of mankind in common, and in such a multitude all the passions of the soul must needs be; though all be not in each.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then cleanse ourselves of these, and so listen to the divine oracles, and with contrite heart hear what has been this day read to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what is that?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “This beginning of miracles did Jesus in Cana of Galilee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I told you the other day, that there are some who say that this is not the beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “For what,” says one, “if ‘Cana of Galilee’ be added?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This shows that this was ‘the beginning’ He made ‘in Cana.’”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But on these points I would not venture to assert anything exactly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I before have shown that He began His miracles after His Baptism, and wrought no miracle before it; but whether of the miracles done after His Baptism, this or some other was the first, it seems to me unnecessary to assert positively.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And manifested forth His glory.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “How?” asks one, “and in what way?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For only the servants, the ruler of the feast, and the bridegroom, not the greater number of those present, gave heed to what was done.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then did he “manifest forth His glory”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He manifested it at least for His own part, and if all present hear not of the miracle at the time, they would hear of it afterwards, for unto the present time it is celebrated, and has not been unnoticed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That all did not know it on the same day is clear from what follows, for after having said that He “manifested forth His glory,” the Evangelist adds,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And His disciples believed on Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: His disciples, who even before this regarded Him with wonder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that it was especially necessary to work the miracles at times when men were present of honest minds, and who would carefully give heed to what was done? for these would more readily believe, and attend more exactly to the circumstances.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And how could He have become known without miracles?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because His doctrine and prophetic powers were sufficient to cause wonder in the souls of His hearers, so that they took heed to what He did with a right disposition, their minds being already well affected towards Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore in many other places the Evangelists say, that He did no miracle on account of the perversity of the men who dwelt there. ( Matt. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 38; ch. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “After this He went down to Capernaum, He, and His mother, and His brethren, and His disciples; and they continued there not many days.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore comes He with “His mother to Capernaum”? for He hath done no miracle there, and the inhabitants of that city were not of those who were rightminded towards Him, but of the utterly corrupt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this Christ declared when He said, “And thou, Capernaum, which are exalted to heaven, shall be thrust down to hell.” ( Luke x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore then goes He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I think it was, because He intended a little after to go up to Jerusalem, that He then went to Capernaum, to avoid leading about everywhere with Him, His mother and His brethren.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so, having departed and tarried a little while to honor His mother, He again commences His miracles after restoring to her home her who had borne Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore the Evangelist says, After “not many days,”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He went up to Jerusalem.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He received baptism then a few days before the passover.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But on going up to Jerusalem, what did He, a deed full of high authority; for He cast out of the Temple those dealers and money changers, and those who sold doves, and oxen, and sheep, and who passed their time there for this purpose.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Another Evangelist writes, that as He cast them out, He said, Make not my Father’s house “a den of thieves,” but this one,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 16. (“Make not My Father’s house) an house of merchandise.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They do not in this contradict each other, but show that he did this a second time, and that both these expressions were not used on the same occasion, but that He acted thus once at the beginning of His ministry, and again when He had come to the very time of His Passion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, (on the latter occasion,) employing more strong expressions, He spoke of it as (being made) “a den of thieves,” but here at the commencement of His miracles He does not so, but uses a more gentle rebuke; from which it is probable that this took place a second time.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And wherefore,” says one, “did Christ do this same, and use such severity against these men, a thing which He is nowhere else seen to do, even when insulted and reviled, and called by them ‘Samaritan’ and ‘demoniac’? for He was not even satisfied with words only, but took a scourge, and so cast them out.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yes, but it was when others were receiving benefit, that the Jews accused and raged against Him; when it was probable that they would have been made savage by His rebukes, they showed no such disposition towards Him, for they neither accused nor reviled Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What say they?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What sign showest Thou unto us, seeing that Thou doest these things?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou their excessive malice, and how the benefits done to others incensed them more (than reproofs)?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At one time then He said, that the Temple was made by them “a den of thieves,” showing that what they sold was gotten by theft, and rapine, and covetousness, and that they were rich through other men’s calamities; at another, “a house of merchandise,” pointing to their shameless traffickings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But wherefore did He this?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since he was about to heal on the Sabbath day, and to do many such things which were thought by them transgressions of the Law, in order that He might not seem to do this as though He had come to be some rival God and opponent of His Father, He takes occasion hence to correct any such suspicion of theirs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For One who had exhibited so much zeal for the House was not likely to oppose Him who was Lord of the House, and who was worshiped in it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: No doubt even the former years during which He lived according to the Law, were sufficient to show His reverence for the Legislator, and that He came not to give contrary laws; yet since it was likely that those years were forgotten through lapse of time, as not having been known to all because He was brought up in a poor and mean dwelling, He afterwards does this in the presence of all, (for many were present because the feast was nigh at hand,) and at great risk.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he did not merely “cast them out,” but also “overturned the tables,” and “poured out the money,” giving them by this to understand, that He who threw Himself into danger for the good order of the House could never despise his Master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had He acted as He did from hypocrisy, He should only have advised them; but to place Himself in danger was very daring.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it was no light thing to offer Himself to the anger of so many market-folk, to excite against Himself a most brutal mob of petty dealers by His reproaches and His blows, this was not the action of a pretender, but of one choosing to suffer everything for the order of the House.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore not by His actions only, but by His words, He shows his agreement with the Father; for He saith not “the Holy House,” but “My Father’s House.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See, He even calls Him, “Father,” and they are not wroth; they thought He spoke in a general way: but when He went on and spoke more plainly, so as to set before them the idea of His Equality, then they become angry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what say they?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What sign showest Thou unto us, seeing that Thou doest these things?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Alas for their utter madness!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Was there need of a sign before they could cease their evil doings, and free the house of God from such dishonor? and was it not the greatest sign of His Excellence that He had gotten such zeal for that House?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In fact, the well-disposed were distinguished by this very thing, for “They,” His disciples, it says,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Remembered that it is written, The zeal of thine house hath eaten me up.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the Jews did not remember the Prophecy, and said, “What sign showest Thou unto us?” ( Ps. lxix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 9 ), both grieving that their shameful traffic was cut off, and expecting by these means to stop Him, and also desiring to challenge Him to a miracle, and to find fault with what He was doing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He will not give them a sign; and before, when they came and asked Him, He made them the same answer, “A wicked and adulterous generation seeketh after a sign; and there shall no sign be given unto it, but the sign of the prophet Jonas.” ( Matt. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Only then the answer was clear, now it is more ambiguous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This He doth on account of their extreme insensibility; for He who prevented them without their asking, and gave them signs, would never when they asked have turned away from them, had He not seen that their minds were wicked and false, and their intention treacherous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Think how full of wickedness the question itself was at the outset.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When they ought to have applauded Him for His earnestness and zeal, when they ought to have been astonished that He cared so greatly for the House, they reproach Him, saying, that it was lawful to traffic, and unlawful for any to stop their traffic, except he should show them a sign.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Destroy this Temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many such sayings He utters which were not intelligible to His immediate hearers, but which were to be so to those that should come after.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And wherefore doth He this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In order that when the accomplishment of His prediction should have come to pass, He might be seen to have foreknown from the beginning what was to follow; which indeed was the case with this prophecy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, saith the Evangelist,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When He was risen from the dead, His disciples remembered that He had said this; and they believed the Scripture, and the word which Jesus had said.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But at the time when this was spoken, the Jews were perplexed as to what it might mean, and cast about to discover, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Forty and six years was this Temple in building, and wilt thou rear it up in three days?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Forty and six years,” they said, referring to the latter building, for the former was finished in twenty years’ time. ( Ezra vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore then did He not resolve the difficulty and say, “I speak not of that Temple, but of My flesh”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why does the Evangelist, writing the Gospel at a later period, interpret the saying, and Jesus keep silence at the time?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why did He so keep silence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because they would not have received His word; for if not even the disciples were able to understand the saying, much less were the multitudes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When,” saith the Evangelist, “He was risen from the dead, then they remembered, and believed the Scripture and His word.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There were two things that hindered them for the time, one the fact of the Resurrection, the other, the greater question whether He was God that dwelt within; of both which things He spake darkly when He said, “Destroy this Temple, and I will rear it up in three days.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this St. Paul declares to be no small proof of His Godhead, when he writes, “Declared to be the Son of God with power, according to the Spirit of holiness, by the Resurrection from the dead.” ( Rom. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why doth He both there, and here, and everywhere, give this for a sign, at one time saying, “When ye have lifted up the Son of Man, then ye shall know that I Am” ( c. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 28 ); at another, “There shall no sign be given you but the sign of the prophet Jonas” ( Matt. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 39 ); and again in this place, “In three days I will raise it up”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because what especially showed that He was not a mere man, was His being able to set up a trophy of victory over death, and so quickly to abolish His long enduring tyranny, and conclude that difficult war.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He saith, “Then ye shall know.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When after My Resurrection I shall draw (all) the world to Me, then ye shall know that I did these things as God, and Very Son of God, avenging the insult offered to My Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why then, instead of saying, ‘What need is there of “signs” to check evil deeds?’ did He promise that He would give them a sign?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because by so doing He would have the more exasperated them; but in this way He rather astonished them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still they made no answer to this, for He seemed to them to say what was incredible, so that they did not stay even to question Him upon it, but passed it by as impossible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet had they been wise, though it seemed to them at the time incredible, still when He wrought His many miracles they would then have come and questioned Him, would then have intreated that the difficulty might be resolved to them; but because they were foolish, they gave no heed at all to part of what was said, and part they heard with evil frame of mind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore Christ spoke to them in an enigmatical way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The question still remains, “How was it that the disciples did not know that He must rise from the dead?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It was, because they had not been vouchsafed the gift of the Spirit; and therefore, though they constantly heard His discourses concerning the Resurrection, they understood them not, but reasoned with themselves what this might be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For very strange and paradoxical was the assertion that one could raise himself, and would raise himself in such wise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And so Peter was rebuked, when, knowing nothing about the Resurrection, he said, “Be it far from Thee.” ( Matt. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Christ did not reveal it clearly to them before the event, that they might not be offended at the very outset, being led to distrust His words on account of the great improbability of the thing, and because they did not yet clearly know Him, who He was.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For no one could help believing what was proclaimed aloud by facts, while some would probably disbelieve what was told to them in words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He at first allowed the meaning of His words to be concealed; but when by their experience He had verified His sayings, He after that gave them understanding of His words, and such gifts of the Spirit that they received them all at once.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He,” saith Jesus, “shall bring all things to your remembrance.” ( c. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they who in a single night cast off all respect for Him, and fled from and denied that they even knew Him, would scarcely have remembered what He had done and said during the whole time, unless they had enjoyed much grace of the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “But,” says one, “if they were to hear from the Spirit, why needed they to accompany Christ when they would not retain His words?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Be cause the Spirit taught them not, but called to their mind what Christ had said before; and it contributes not a little to the glory of Christ, that they were referred to the remembrance of the words He had spoken to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At the first then it was of the gift of God that the grace of the Spirit lighted upon them so largely and abundantly; but after that, it was of their own virtue that they retained the Gift.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they displayed a shining life, and much wisdom, and great labors, and despised this present life, and thought nothing of earthly things, but were above them all; and like a sort of light-winged eagle, soaring high by their works; reached to heaven itself, and by these possessed the unspeakable grace of the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then imitate them, and not quench our lamps, but keep them bright by alms-doing, for so is the light of this fire preserved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us collect the oil into our vessels whilst we are here, for we cannot buy it when we have departed to that other place, nor can we procure it elsewhere, save only at the hands of the poor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us therefore collect it thence very abundantly, if, at least, we desire to enter in with the Bridegroom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if we do not this, we must remain without the bridechamber, for it is impossible, it is impossible, though we perform ten thousand other good deeds, to enter the portals of the Kingdom without alms-doing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then show forth this very abundantly, that we may enjoy those ineffable blessings; which may it come to pass that we all attain, by the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, for ever and ever.
